description_long: This is a classic chessboard with a square playing surface divided into an 8x8 grid of alternating light and dark squares. The board is framed by a thick, wooden border with a rich, brown wood grain texture, giving it a sturdy and elegant appearance. The chessboard is set up for play, with black and white chess pieces arranged in their standard starting positions. The chess pieces are traditional Staunton-style, with distinct shapes for each type, and are made of contrasting black and white materials. The surface of the board is smooth and slightly glossy, reflecting light subtly. The overall construction suggests a solid, well-crafted object suitable for both indoor and outdoor urban environments such as parks or public squares.
description: A wooden chessboard with a glossy 8x8 grid of alternating light and dark squares, bordered by a thick, brown wood frame and set up with classic black and white chess pieces.
description_view_0: The chessboard is viewed from the white side, showing all white pieces in the foreground and black pieces in the background, with the wood border clearly visible.
description_view_1: The board is seen from the left side, displaying the full lineup of both black and white pieces along the edge, emphasizing the depth of the wood border.
description_view_2: The chessboard is viewed from the black side, with black pieces in the foreground and white pieces in the background, highlighting the alternating square pattern.
description_view_3: The board is seen from the right side, showing the side profiles of both sets of pieces and the thickness of the wooden border.
attribute_board_pattern: The playing surface features a regular grid of 64 squares arranged in an 8x8 pattern, with alternating light and dark squares. The squares are perfectly aligned and evenly sized, creating a classic checkerboard appearance. The border is plain and unadorned, emphasizing the contrast between the playing area and the frame.
attribute_border_design: The border is a wide, solid band of polished brown wood with a visible grain pattern. It is smooth, unornamented, and slightly raised above the playing surface, providing a sturdy frame with no visible inscriptions or decorative markings.
